core_concepts: Push-Pull-Legs (PPL) Split: High-frequency, dedicated training sessions for chest, shoulders, and triceps.
Hypertrophy Focus: Maximizing muscle growth through exercise selection, mechanical tension, and metabolic stress.
Mechanical Tension: Utilizing heavy loads on compound movements for primary stimulus.
End-Range Emphasis: Targeting muscles in fully lengthened (stretched) and fully contracted positions.
Inter-set Stretching: Holding stretches during rest periods to potentially enhance muscular adaptations.
Partial Reps (Stretched vs. Squeezed): Using specific partial ranges to emphasize target tissues where they are strongest or most stimulated.
Anatomical Variation: Altering joint angles and planes of motion to target different muscular compartments.
Progressive Overload: Systematic increase in intensity and volume over time.

actionable_advice.warm_up: 5 minutes cardio + upper body dynamic drills (arm circles, cable external rotations).

bench_press.form: Stable base (butt, upper back, head), pinched shoulder blades, controlled negative, explosive positive.
bench_press.elbow_position: 0-30 degrees of tuck for shoulder health and pectoral alignment.

larson_press.intensity: 75% of bench press top set weight.
larson_press.form: Legs elevated to eliminate leg drive, controlled tempo (1-2s negative, 1-2s positive).

dumbbell_arnold_press.form: Palm-in to flare-out motion, maintain core tightness, use belt/straps for heavier loading.

inter_set_stretching.protocol: 30-second static hold at 7/10 intensity in between sets of cable press-arounds.

triceps_supersets.technique: Superset squeeze-only press-downs (second half of ROM) with stretch-only overhead extensions (first half of ROM).

inter_set_stretching.description: Studies suggest 30-second static stretching at moderate intensity between sets may increase muscle growth.

static_stretching_interference.description: Static stretching does not interfere with strength unless held for >60-90 seconds.

deltoid_anatomy.description: The deltoid may be composed of seven distinct intramuscular segments (e.g., D4, D5) rather than just three heads.

triceps_long_head.description: The long head crosses both shoulder and elbow joints; varying shoulder position influences its activation.

coaching_insights: Prioritize stability on heavy compound lifts to maximize force production.
When time is limited, consider collapsing horizontal and vertical pushing into single compound movements, though dedicated movements are better for specialization.
The 'sticking point' or hardest portion of a movement is not always the best place to focus; balancing stretch-focused training is vital for hypertrophy.
Don't ignore the end-range contraction (e.g., passing the midline in fly/press movements) if it can be achieved without compromising the stretch.
Utilizing 'stretched' vs 'squeezed' partials can be a powerful tool for overcoming training plateaus or adding intensity without excessive systemic fatigue.
common_mistakes: Yanking the bar off the rack rather than having a spotter lift it out smoothly.
Ignoring the depth requirements (elbow below shoulder) for legal/standard bench press form.
Turning overhead presses or lateral raises into momentum-based swings.
Focusing only on the 'pump' (mind-muscle connection) while ignoring progressive loading on key compound movements.
Performing partial reps randomly instead of strategically (e.g., specifically targeting stretched vs. squeezed phases).

motivational_quotes: Train the range of motion, grow the muscle.
Don't just move weight, control the tension.
The stretch is where the growth happens.
Master the basics, then optimize the details.
Consistent progress is the only path to success.
Training isn't just about the set; it's about what you do in between.
Find the angle that makes your muscles work, not your joints ache.
Every repetition is an opportunity for adaptation.
myths_addressed: You need a massive powerlifting arch to bench heavy.
Partial reps are 'ego lifting' and useless.
You must stop at the midline for all chest exercises.
Heavy weight training is the only way to grow.
Triceps only care about elbow extension.
Pressing must be done with elbows tucked at 0 degrees.
Mind-muscle connection is more important than load progression.
You need to change your exercises every week to confuse the muscles.

grit_application.implementation_strategy: The app should utilize a 'Dynamic Hypertrophy' module. 1. Users input their primary goal (e.g., '3D Shoulders' or 'Massive Chest'). 2. The app auto-adjusts exercise selection based on the biomechanical principles highlighted (e.g., suggesting crossbody Y-raises instead of standard lateral raises). 3. It incorporates 'Inter-set Stretches' into the rest timers to keep users productive. 4. It includes a tracking feature for 'Top Set' vs 'Back-off Set' performance, ensuring the user hits the necessary RPE (near-max) before switching to controlled volume work. 5. 'Stretched/Squeezed Partial' coaching cues are integrated directly into the exercise animations to ensure proper execution.
